title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/term-insurance-for-single-mother.html
Term Insurance for Single Mother
Losing a loved one takes a financial toll on a family despite their gender. Today, women, especially single mothers, are increasingly balancing both emotional and financial responsibilities for their families. In such a scenario, term insurance becomes an essential financial safety net for single mothers .  Read More
What Is Term Insurance for Single Mothers?  A term insurance plan is a  life insurance  policy taken for a specific period, offering financial protection to the life assured’s family in case of their death during that period. If something unfortunate happens to the life assured, the insurance company provides a death benefit to their beneficiaries—usually their family members. For a single mother, this financial security is even more crucial. Term insurance for single mothers ensures that their children and dependents are financially protected in the event of an untimely death. A single-mother-term plan helps to safeguard the family’s financial future and cover important expenses, providing peace of mind to the mother knowing her loved ones are secure.  [2]
How Does Term Insurance for Single Mothers Work?
Single mothers often face unique financial challenges, especially when they become the sole breadwinner due to unfortunate circumstances like the death of a spouse or divorce. Term insurance serves as a safety net, providing high coverage at affordable premiums to secure the financial future of their children. Major insurers offer term plans catering to women, with options to add  critical illness riders  that cover conditions such as cancer. [3]
Purchasing a standalone term insurance plan allows single mothers to safeguard their families financially. Despite the hesitation many women feel about consulting financial planners, professional guidance can empower them to make smarter financial decisions, ensuring long-term stability and resilience. [3] [5]
Need For Term Insurance For Single Mother?   Single mothers today juggle many roles—caregiver, provider, and protector of their family’s future. While managing a household, single mothers work tirelessly to ensure their children’s needs are met and their future is secure. However, when it comes to  financial planning  , many overlook the importance of securing their own future. This is where term insurance becomes essential.
Protection for Your Family  As a single mother, you are the sole provider for your children and dependents. Protecting them during unforeseen circumstances is crucial.  Term insurance  can give you peace of mind, knowing that your family will have the financial support they need, even if you are no longer there to provide for them.
Covering Essential Expenses   From everyday household expenses to saving for your children's education or special events, as a single mother, you need to manage all the finances. Term insurance allows you to plan ahead and put money aside for these important expenses, ensuring your family's needs are met without worry.
Long-term Financial Goals  If you have big goals like buying a home or starting a business, a term insurance plan can help you save and protect your family’s future. Some policies even offer  investment options   that can align with your long-term goals, providing both security and financial growth opportunities.
Preparing for Retirement  Relying solely on one person’s retirement savings can be risky. As a single mother, it’s important to have your own  retirement plan   in place. With term insurance, you can ensure that you’re financially prepared for the future, especially with rising medical expenses in old age.
Leaving a Legacy for Your Children  Finally, term insurance allows you to leave behind a legacy for your children. Even though you may not always be there to guide them, you can provide a financial cushion to help them navigate life. Knowing that your children are secure financially is the greatest gift you can give them.
For single mothers, securing term insurance is not just about financial protection—it’s about ensuring that your family remains strong, supported, and cared for, no matter what the future holds.
Benefits Of Term Insurance For Single Mothers
Term insurance offers numerous benefits to women, providing both financial protection and peace of mind. Here’s why women should consider getting a term insurance plan:
Adaptability to Life’s Changes   As a woman, your financial responsibilities evolve over time. Whether you are single, married, or have children, term insurance allows you to adjust your coverage to meet your changing needs. You can easily update beneficiaries, such as adding your spouse or children, and increase your coverage when significant life milestones, like marriage or parenthood, occur. This ensures that your insurance keeps pace with your life’s changes. [1]  Affordable and Accessible Coverage   Term insurance is known for being budget-friendly, and women often benefit from lower premiums due to their longer life expectancy. Due to their longer life expectancy, insurers offer lower rates, making term insurance more affordable for women in various financial situations, from young professionals to self-employed individuals.  [1]  High Coverage for Financial Responsibilities  Single mothers juggle multiple roles in their families, balancing both financial contributions and household duties. A term insurance plan with a high sum assured ensures that your family’s financial needs—like household expenses and children’s education—are covered if something happens to you. It provides the financial security needed for those who depend on you.  [1]  Emotional and Financial Support   Term insurance offers more than just financial assistance; it provides peace of mind. The death benefit can help families navigate difficult times by ensuring their financial needs are met. This support can bring emotional comfort, helping them focus on healing while maintaining financial stability.  [1]  Protection Against Debts  If you have outstanding loans, such as a home or car loan, term insurance becomes essential. In the event of your absence, these debts could become a burden on your loved ones. A term insurance plan that covers your loan amount ensures that your family won’t face financial strain due to these obligations.  [1]  Tax Benefits for Additional Savings  One of the lesser-known  benefits of term insurance   is the potential for tax savings. Women can claim tax deductions on premiums paid up to ₹1.5 lakh  under Section 80C   (in case of old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act. Additionally, the payout from the policy to your nominee is also tax-exempt under Section 10(10D), further adding to the financial advantages.  [1]   How to Choose the Right Term Plan for Women    Coverage Amount   Women should carefully evaluate their financial responsibilities, such as outstanding loans and future obligations. Additionally, women enjoy up to 30% lower premiums compared to men, making term insurance more affordable.  [6]  Assessing Your Stage of Life  Coverage requirements change over time. In your 20s, a lesser amount might suffice, but by the time you reach 35-40, your responsibilities increase, hence a higher coverage is recommended. Adding a Life Stage Benefit rider offers flexibility, allowing you to increase coverage as responsibilities grow. For those nearing retirement, term plans with an exit option provide value for money, even if the coverage isn’t fully utilized .  [6]  Riders for Critical Illnesses  Riders for critical illnesses, such as cancer, offer additional coverage to help manage medical expenses and compensate for potential income loss due to illness. This extra protection can be vital for women, considering the increasing prevalence of lifestyle-related diseases.  [6]
How To Buy Term Insurance For Single Parent(Father or Mother)?
Assess Your Family’s Needs and Finances  Evaluate your family’s financial situation and needs before deciding on the policy amount. This helps ensure that the coverage is adequate to protect your loved ones.  [4]  Recommended Coverage Amount  A good guideline is to set the insured amount at 15-20 times your annual income. This will help provide sufficient financial security for your family in case of unforeseen circumstances.  [4]  Compare Different Plans  Before purchasing a plan, take the time to  compare term plans  online. This will allow you to find the plan that offers the best value and meets your needs effectively.  [4]  Consider Add-On Riders   Look for a plan that offers add-on riders to extend coverage. These riders can help enhance your policy by providing additional protection, such as critical illness cover or  accidental death benefits , on payment of nominal additional premium.  [4]
Conclusion
For a single mother, protecting her child's future is a priority. Term insurance for single mothers offers an effective and affordable way to provide financial security in case of an unforeseen event. By  choosing the right term plan , you can ensure that your child will be taken care of, even in your absence. With benefits such as low premiums, high coverage, tax advantages, and the option to add riders, a single-mother term plan is an investment that provides peace of mind for both you and your family.
Frequently Asked Questions    Why is term insurance important for single mothers?    Term insurance   provides financial security, ensuring your child’s needs are met in case of your untimely passing.
What are the riders I can add to my term plan for single mothers?    Riders such as  critical illness, accidental death, and  waiver of premium riders   can be added for extra protection, on payment of nominal additional premium.
What are the things to consider before buying a term insurance plan for single mothers?    Consider the coverage amount, premium costs,  policy term , and available riders before purchasing.
What are the premium payment options for a single mother term insurance plan?    Premium payments  can typically be made monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annually, based on your preference.
How much coverage should a single mother have in term insurance?  It’s generally recommended to have coverage 15-20 times your annual income  4 .
Can I change the nominee in my term insurance policy?  Yes, you can update the nominee whenever needed.
What happens if I outlive my term insurance policy?  The policy expires without a payout, but some plans offer renewal options. However, some insurance plans offer ROP [Return of Premium]. You can purchase a term plan with  Return of Premium  which will provide you death benefit as well as maturity benefit by returning the premium invested, in case you outlive the policy.
Is the sum assured from term insurance taxable?  No, the death benefit is generally tax-free under Section 10(10D).
Can I get term insurance without a medical exam?  Some plans offer coverage without a medical exam, depending on your age and health status.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com)carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) Reg. Office Address: Bajaj Insurance House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006. CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959,  call us on Customer Care No. 020-6712 1212 , mail us on: customercare@bajajlife.com. The Logo of Bajaj Life Insurance Limited is provided on the basis of license given by Bajaj Finserv Ltd. to use its “Bajaj” Logo.
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 10(10D) and Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy

Term Insurance for Single Mother
Losing a loved one takes a financial toll on a family despite their gender. Today, women, especially single mothers, are increasingly balancing both emotional and financial responsibilities for their families. In such a scenario, term insurance becomes an essential financial safety net for single mothers . Read More
What Is Term Insurance for Single Mothers? A term insurance plan is a life insurance policy taken for a specific period, offering financial protection to the life assured’s family in case of their death during that period. If something unfortunate happens to the life assured, the insurance company provides a death benefit to their beneficiaries—usually their family members. For a single mother, this financial security is even more crucial. Term insurance for single mothers ensures that their children and dependents are financially protected in the event of an untimely death. A single-mother-term plan helps to safeguard the family’s financial future and cover important expenses, providing peace of mind to the mother knowing her loved ones are secure. [2]
How Does Term Insurance for Single Mothers Work?
Single mothers often face unique financial challenges, especially when they become the sole breadwinner due to unfortunate circumstances like the death of a spouse or divorce. Term insurance serves as a safety net, providing high coverage at affordable premiums to secure the financial future of their children. Major insurers offer term plans catering to women, with options to add critical illness riders that cover conditions such as cancer. [3]
Purchasing a standalone term insurance plan allows single mothers to safeguard their families financially. Despite the hesitation many women feel about consulting financial planners, professional guidance can empower them to make smarter financial decisions, ensuring long-term stability and resilience. [3] [5]
Need For Term Insurance For Single Mother? Single mothers today juggle many roles—caregiver, provider, and protector of their family’s future. While managing a household, single mothers work tirelessly to ensure their children’s needs are met and their future is secure. However, when it comes to financial planning ,
many overlook the importance of securing their own future. This is where term insurance becomes essential.
Protection for Your Family As a single mother, you are the sole provider for your children and dependents. Protecting them during unforeseen circumstances is crucial. Term insurance can give you peace of mind, knowing that your family will have the financial support they need, even if you are no longer there to provide for them.
Covering Essential Expenses From everyday household expenses to saving for your children's education or special events, as a single mother, you need to manage all the finances. Term insurance allows you to plan ahead and put money aside for these important expenses, ensuring your family's needs are met without worry.
Long-term Financial Goals If you have big goals like buying a home or starting a business, a term insurance plan can help you save and protect your family’s future. Some policies even offer investment options that can align with your long-term goals, providing both security and financial growth opportunities.
Preparing for Retirement Relying solely on one person’s retirement savings can be risky. As a single mother, it’s important to have your own retirement plan in place. With term insurance, you can ensure that you’re financially prepared for the future, especially with rising medical expenses in old age.
Leaving a Legacy for Your Children Finally, term insurance allows you to leave behind a legacy for your children. Even though you may not always be there to guide them, you can provide a financial cushion to help them navigate life. Knowing that your children are secure financially is the greatest gift you can give them.
For single mothers, securing term insurance is not just about financial protection—it’s about ensuring that your family remains strong, supported, and cared for, no matter what the future holds.
Benefits Of Term Insurance For Single Mothers
Term insurance offers numerous benefits to women, providing both financial protection and peace of mind. Here’s why women should consider getting a term insurance plan:
Adaptability to Life’s Changes
As a woman, your financial responsibilities evolve over time. Whether you are single, married, or have children, term insurance allows you to adjust your coverage to meet your changing needs. You can easily update beneficiaries, such as adding your spouse or children, and increase your coverage when significant life milestones, like marriage or parenthood, occur. This ensures that your insurance keeps pace with your life’s changes. [1] Affordable and Accessible Coverage
Term insurance is known for being budget-friendly, and women often benefit from lower premiums due to their longer life expectancy. Due to their longer life expectancy, insurers offer lower rates, making term insurance more affordable for women in various financial situations, from young professionals to self-employed individuals. [1] High Coverage for Financial Responsibilities
Single mothers juggle multiple roles in their families, balancing both financial contributions and household duties. A term insurance plan with a high sum assured ensures that your family’s financial needs—like household expenses and children’s education—are covered if something happens to you. It provides the financial security needed for those who depend on you. [1] Emotional and Financial Support
Term insurance offers more than just financial assistance; it provides peace of mind. The death benefit can help families navigate difficult times by ensuring their financial needs are met. This support can bring emotional comfort, helping them focus on healing while maintaining financial stability. [1] Protection Against Debts
If you have outstanding loans, such as a home or car loan, term insurance becomes essential. In the event of your absence, these debts could become a burden on your loved ones. A term insurance plan that covers your loan amount ensures that your family won’t face financial strain due to these obligations. [1] Tax Benefits for Additional Savings
One of the lesser-known benefits of term insurance is the potential for tax savings. Women
can claim tax deductions on premiums paid up to ₹1.5 lakh under Section 80C (in case of old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act. Additionally, the payout from the policy to your nominee is also tax-exempt under Section 10(10D), further adding to the financial advantages. [1]
How to Choose the Right Term Plan for Women
Coverage Amount
Women should carefully evaluate their financial responsibilities, such as outstanding loans and future obligations. Additionally, women enjoy up to 30% lower premiums compared to men, making term insurance more affordable. [6] Assessing Your Stage of Life
Coverage requirements change over time. In your 20s, a lesser amount might suffice, but by the time you reach 35-40, your responsibilities increase, hence a higher coverage is recommended. Adding a Life Stage Benefit rider offers flexibility, allowing you to increase coverage as responsibilities grow. For those nearing retirement, term plans with an exit option provide value for money, even if the coverage isn’t fully utilized . [6] Riders for Critical Illnesses Riders for critical illnesses, such as cancer, offer additional coverage to help manage medical expenses and compensate for potential income loss due to illness. This extra protection can be vital for women, considering the increasing prevalence of lifestyle-related diseases. [6]
How To Buy Term Insurance For Single Parent(Father or Mother)?
Assess Your Family’s Needs and Finances
Evaluate your family’s financial situation and needs before deciding on the policy amount. This helps ensure that the coverage is adequate to protect your loved ones. [4] Recommended Coverage Amount
A good guideline is to set the insured amount at 15-20 times your annual income. This will help provide sufficient financial security for your family in case of unforeseen circumstances. [4] Compare Different Plans
Before purchasing a plan, take the time to compare term plans online. This will allow you to find the plan that offers the best value and meets your needs effectively. [4] Consider Add-On Riders
Look for a plan that offers add-on riders to extend coverage. These riders can help enhance
your policy by providing additional protection, such as critical illness cover or accidental death benefits , on payment of nominal additional premium. [4]
Conclusion
For a single mother, protecting her child's future is a priority. Term insurance for single mothers offers an effective and affordable way to provide financial security in case of an unforeseen event. By choosing the right term plan , you can ensure that your child will be taken care of, even in your absence. With benefits such as low premiums, high coverage, tax advantages, and the option to add riders, a single-mother term plan is an investment that provides peace of mind for both you and your family.
Frequently Asked Questions
Why is term insurance important for single mothers? Term insurance provides financial security, ensuring your child’s needs are met in case of your untimely passing.
What are the riders I can add to my term plan for single mothers? Riders such as critical illness, accidental death, and waiver of premium riders can be added for extra protection, on payment of nominal additional premium.
What are the things to consider before buying a term insurance plan for single mothers? Consider the coverage amount, premium costs, policy term , and available riders before purchasing.
What are the premium payment options for a single mother term insurance plan? Premium payments can typically be made monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annually, based on your preference.
How much coverage should a single mother have in term insurance? It’s generally recommended to have coverage 15-20 times your annual income 4 .
Can I change the nominee in my term insurance policy? Yes, you can update the nominee whenever needed.
What happens if I outlive my term insurance policy? The policy expires without a payout, but some plans offer renewal options. However, some insurance plans offer ROP [Return of Premium]. You can purchase a term plan with Return of Premium which will provide you death benefit as well as maturity benefit by returning the premium invested, in case you outlive the policy.
Is the sum assured from term insurance taxable? No, the death benefit is generally tax-free under Section 10(10D).
Can I get term insurance without a medical exam? Some plans offer coverage without a medical exam, depending on your age and health status.

Term insurance provides financial security, ensuring your child’s needs are met in case of your untimely passing.

changes.word_original: Riders such as  critical illness, accidental death, and  waiver of premium riders   can be added for extra protection, on payment of nominal additional premium.
changes.pdf_final: Riders such as critical illness, accidental death, and waiver of premium riders can be added for extra protection, on payment of nominal additional premium.

changes.word_original: Consider the coverage amount, premium costs,  policy term , and available riders before purchasing.
changes.pdf_final: Consider the coverage amount, premium costs, policy term , and available riders before purchasing.

changes.word_original: Premium payments  can typically be made monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annually, based on your preference.
changes.pdf_final: Premium payments can typically be made monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annually, based on your preference.

changes.word_original: It’s generally recommended to have coverage 15-20 times your annual income  4 .
changes.pdf_final: It’s generally recommended to have coverage 15-20 times your annual income 4 .

changes.word_original: You can purchase a term plan with  Return of Premium  which will provide you death benefit as well as maturity benefit by returning the premium invested, in case you outlive the policy.
